name: Mississippi SNAP Benefits
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing monthly food assistance to eligible low-income Mississippi residents through an EBT card.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Mississippi Medicaid (DOM)
description: State Medicaid program administered by the Division of Medicaid providing health coverage for low-income children, families, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled Mississippians.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Children up to 209% FPL; pregnant women up to 194% FPL; aged/disabled at SSI levels

name: Mississippi Department of Employment Security - Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial benefits to workers who have lost their jobs through no fault of their own while they actively seek new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Mississippi Home Corporation - Housing Assistance
description: State housing finance agency providing affordable mortgage programs, down payment assistance, rental assistance, and housing counseling for Mississippians.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally up to 80% area median income

name: Mississippi Center for Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in southern Mississippi for housing, family, consumer, and benefits cases.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of southern Mississippi counties
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: North Mississippi Rural Legal Services
description: Free legal aid for low-income residents in northern Mississippi covering family law, housing, consumer debt, employment, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of northern Mississippi counties
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Mississippi WIN Job Centers
description: State workforce development centers providing job search assistance, career counseling, skills training, and employer connections at locations statewide.
eligibilitySummary: Any Mississippi resident seeking employment services

name: Mississippi LIHEAP - Home Energy Assistance
description: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helping eligible households pay heating and cooling costs through the Mississippi Department of Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy costs
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% of federal poverty level

name: Mississippi 211
description: Free information and referral service connecting Mississippians to local health and human services resources including food, housing, and utility assistance.
eligibilitySummary: Any Mississippi resident seeking help or information about available services

name: Mississippi Food Network
description: Feeding America affiliate and the state's largest food bank distributing food to hundreds of partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and soup kitchens across Mississippi.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of food assistance across Mississippi

name: Mississippi State Veterans Affairs Board
description: Assists Mississippi veterans and their dependents with filing claims, accessing benefits, and connecting to state and federal VA programs and services.
eligibilitySummary: Mississippi veterans and their families

name: Mississippi Crisis Line - CONTACT Helpline
description: Statewide crisis helpline providing emotional support, crisis intervention, suicide prevention, and referrals to community resources.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Mississippi experiencing a mental health crisis or emotional distress

name: Mississippi Child Care Payment Program
description: Childcare subsidy program helping low-income working families, TANF recipients, and families in training pay for childcare services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families, TANF recipients, and families in approved training
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% of state median income

name: Mississippi TANF Cash Assistance
description: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families providing cash benefits and supportive services to very low-income families with children to promote self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children
incomeLimitNotes: Income must be below TANF payment standard for family size

name: Mississippi WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children program providing supplemental foods, nutrition counseling, and healthcare referrals for pregnant women, new mothers, and young children.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum women; infants and children under 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Mississippi CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Provides low-cost health coverage for uninsured children in families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income too high for Medicaid
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 209% of federal poverty level

name: COAST Transit Authority - Jackson Metro Area
description: Public transportation system serving the Jackson metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and demand-response paratransit.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Jackson metro area; reduced fare for seniors and disabled riders

name: Jackson-Hinds Comprehensive Health Center
description: Federally qualified health center in Jackson providing affordable primary care, dental, behavioral health, and pharmacy services regardless of ability to pay.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in the Jackson area; sliding fee scale for uninsured patients
incomeLimitNotes: Sliding fee scale based on income and family size

name: Mississippi Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency upgrades to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating/cooling system repairs to reduce utility costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Mississippi Adult Education / GED Program
description: Free adult basic education, GED preparation, and English language classes for adults across Mississippi seeking to improve their education and employment prospects.
eligibilitySummary: Adults age 17 and older not enrolled in K-12 school

name: Mississippi Volunteer Lawyers Project
description: Pro bono legal assistance program matching low-income Mississippians with volunteer attorneys for civil legal matters including family, housing, and consumer issues.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Mississippi residents with civil legal needs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Mississippi Department of Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living services, and disability determination for Mississippians with disabilities seeking employment.
eligibilitySummary: Mississippi residents with physical or mental disabilities that impact employment

name: Mississippi Division of Aging and Adult Services
description: Provides services for seniors including home-delivered meals, homemaker services, respite care, and adult day care to help elderly Mississippians remain independent.
eligibilitySummary: Mississippi residents age 60 and older
incomeLimitNotes: Some services have income requirements; others available to all seniors

name: Gulf Coast Community Action Agency
description: Community action agency serving the Mississippi Gulf Coast with emergency assistance, housing counseling, Head Start, and workforce development programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income residents of the Mississippi Gulf Coast region
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Mississippi Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis support, safety planning, and shelter referrals for survivors of domestic violence throughout Mississippi.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of domestic violence in Mississippi

name: Mississippi Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers
description: Federally funded rental assistance vouchers administered through local housing authorities helping very low-income families afford safe housing.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals
incomeLimitNotes: Generally household income at or below 50% of area median income

name: Mississippi Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounted phone and broadband service to eligible low-income Mississippians to ensure access to essential telecommunications services.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal program

name: Mississippi Community College Board - Workforce Training
description: Coordinates workforce training programs through community colleges across the state including short-term certificates, apprenticeships, and customized employer training.
eligibilitySummary: Adults seeking workforce training and career education
incomeLimitNotes: Financial aid available for qualifying students

name: Salvation Army Mississippi - Emergency Assistance
description: Provides emergency assistance including food, rent and utility help, clothing, and disaster relief across Mississippi communities.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families in need of emergency assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program and location

name: Jackson Housing Authority
description: Provides public housing and rental assistance to low-income families, elderly, and disabled residents in the Jackson metropolitan area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in the Jackson area
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 80% area median income
